publications.title: Structural insights into engineering a T-cell receptor targeting MAGE-A10 with higher affinity and specificity for cancer immunotherapy.
publications.authors: Simister PC, Border EC, Vieira JF, Pumphrey NJ.
publications.executive_summary: This study presents crystal structures of parental and engineered T-cell receptors (TCRs) bound to HLA-A*02:01 presenting the MAGE-A10 9-mer peptide (GLYDGMEHL), a melanoma-associated antigen targeted for TCR immunotherapy. A single Glu31-Asp mutation in the β-chain CDR1 of the parental TCR increased affinity while maintaining selectivity, an improvement revealed by high-resolution crystal structures showing that the shorter aspartate residue shifts the TCR β-chain domain and creates localized changes at the peptide C-terminus. The structures explain differential cross-reactivity between two clinical candidate TCRs through variations in surface electrostatics and the shape and size of TCR-peptide interfacial cavities—molecular features that constrain recognition of structurally similar MAGE-A10 variants. These findings highlight how modest structural changes in engineered TCRs can simultaneously enhance affinity and reduce off-target cross-reactivity, properties critical for improving the safety and efficacy of TCR-based cancer immunotherapy in clinical use.
